analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 8 finding(s) from 8 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: The advanced debug interface exposes an external JTAG/TAP controlled AXI master path without an observed debug authorization, lifecycle-enable, or address-range policy guard at the module/top-level ports or at AXI valid generation.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write/trigger AXI transactions / AXI-addressable regions reachable through axi_master_aw/ar/w channels / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: external JTAG/TAP requester on tms_pad_i/tck_pad_i/trstn_pad_i/tdi_pad_i Operation: read/write/trigger AXI transactions Object: AXI-addressable regions reachable through axi_master_aw/ar/w channels Expected guard: debug authentication/authorization or lifecycle/debug-enable guard before issuing AXI master transactions; address-range/access-policy mediation for protected SoC regions Observed behavior: adv_dbg_if instantiates adbg_top and connects pad/TAP-derived signals to adbg_top; adbg_top selects the AXI module using debug_select_i, module_id_reg and module_selects; adbg_axi_biu drives axi_master_aw_valid/ar_valid/w_valid from its FSM when start_toggle is observed, with no observed auth/range input in the inspected path. Repository searches for debug_enable, lifecycle, and auth did not identify a guard signal other than copyright/author text. Path: JTAG pads -> adv_dbg_if.cluster_tap_i/adbg_tap_top -> adbg_top -> adbg_axi_module -> adbg_axi_biu -> axi_master_aw/ar/w Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: If the AXI master is connected to the SoC fabric, an external JTAG actor can initiate debug-originated reads/writes that may bypass normal CPU privilege checks and reach protected memory/MMIO.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full top-level SoC connection of the adv_dbg_if AXI master to the system fabric/address map was not fully present in the provided observations.

findings.summary: The LINT JTAG wrapper connects TAP-selected debug logic directly to a XBAR_TCDM_BUS master without an observed authorization/lifecycle guard or address-range mediation on lint_req/lint_add/lint_wen.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write through LINT debug bus / LINT/TCDM bus address space and connected memories/peripherals / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: external JTAG/TAP debug subject Operation: read/write through LINT debug bus Object: LINT/TCDM bus address space and connected memories/peripherals Expected guard: debug authorization/lifecycle enable and address-range mediation before lint/TCDM read/write requests Observed behavior: lint_jtag_wrap instantiates adbg_lintonly_top and maps lint_req_o/add/wen/wdata/be directly to jtag_lint_master. adbg_lint_biu latches addr/data/write on strobe_i && rdy_o and later issues LINT requests; no wrapper or BIU auth/range guard was observed. Path: JTAG/TAP -> lint_jtag_wrap -> adbg_lintonly_top/adbg_lint_module -> adbg_lint_biu -> jtag_lint_master XBAR_TCDM_BUS Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: External JTAG-selected LINT transactions may access TCDM/L2/debug bus resources without lifecycle authorization or address policy enforcement.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Detailed downstream address map and protected-region classification were not provided.

findings.summary: TAP and debug modules expose TDO paths, and adbg_axi_biu/adbg_lint_biu capture bus read data into data_out_reg before shifting through module_tdo_o. Observed reset clears some readback registers. However, provided observations do not include complete TDO mux logic or enough deselect-transition evidence to prove or disprove stale-data leakage.
findings.vulnerability_category: expose/read via TDO shift path / debug module read data, bus response data, status/control registers shifted to TDO / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: external JTAG/TAP shift requester Operation: expose/read via TDO shift path Object: debug module read data, bus response data, status/control registers shifted to TDO Expected guard: TAP instruction access to debug modules should require debug authorization or lifecycle-enable when protected data or bus access is exposed; inactive/unselected modules must not leak stale protected read data on TDO Observed behavior: TAP and debug modules expose TDO paths, and adbg_axi_biu/adbg_lint_biu capture bus read data into data_out_reg before shifting through module_tdo_o. Observed reset clears some readback registers. However, provided observations do not include complete TDO mux logic or enough deselect-transition evidence to prove or disprove stale-data leakage. Path: TAP instruction/data registers -> debug_select/module_select -> module_tdo_o/debug TDO mux -> external tdo Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Potential exposure of protected bus read data on TDO remains possible if bus reachability is protected and stale readback registers are not cleared on deselect.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Complete adbg_top/adbg_tap_top TDO mux lines and module deselect/readback clear logic were not fully included. Protected nature of shifted data depends on bus reachability.

findings.summary: Debug command execution is gated by TAP/module state and CRC/integrity logic, but no independent debug authorization or lifecycle guard was observed at the transaction trigger/use site for AXI or LINT bus operations.
findings.vulnerability_category: configure/trigger bus read or write / debug BIU transaction-control state, address register, data register, strobe / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: JTAG serial command requester Operation: configure/trigger bus read or write Object: debug BIU transaction-control state, address register, data register, strobe Expected guard: command execution should be gated by valid module selection, CRC/command integrity if implemented, and debug authorization; malformed or partial scan updates must not trigger protected bus operations Observed behavior: adbg_axi_module and adbg_lint_module implement serial command decode, address/operation registers, CRC logic, and BIU strobe paths. The transaction trigger path ultimately uses strobe_i/start_toggle in the BIU. No auth/lifecycle signal was present in module ports, wrapper ports, or search results. Path: JTAG scan data/update_dr -> adbg_axi_module/adbg_lint_module command FSM -> BIU strobe/address/data -> AXI/LINT bus Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A valid JTAG command sequence can trigger bus reads/writes based on TAP selection/integrity only, without permission authorization for protected bus operations.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The exact command FSM transition lines around BIU strobe generation were only partially observed, so malformed/partial-scan handling was not fully assessed.

findings.summary: The advanced debug top exposes CPU debug control/status interfaces and OR1K debug modules are present, but no authorization/lifecycle guard was observed on CPU debug control access from TAP selection alone.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write/control CPU debug state / CPU debug control/status, stall/reset/debug bus access / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: external JTAG/TAP OR1K debug requester Operation: read/write/control CPU debug state Object: CPU debug control/status, stall/reset/debug bus access Expected guard: processor debug control and status access should require debug authorization/lifecycle enable; writes that halt, reset, or modify CPU-visible state should not be possible from unauthenticated TAP selection alone Observed behavior: adv_dbg_if and adbg_top expose CPU debug outputs including cpu_stall_o, cpu_stb_o, cpu_we_o, cpu_rst_o and input cpu_data/cpu_ack signals. Static facts include adbg_or1k_module, adbg_or1k_biu, and adbg_or1k_status_reg. No auth/lifecycle guard signal was found in searches or visible ports. Path: JTAG/TAP -> adbg_top CPU/OR1K debug module path -> cpu_stall_o/cpu_rst_o/cpu_stb_o/cpu_we_o and CPU debug data/address signals Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: If connected to CPU debug controls, an external TAP actor may halt/reset/control or observe CPU debug state without authorization.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The provided adbg_top excerpt is truncated before the full CPU/OR1K module instantiation; actual SoC integration/connectivity of CPU debug outputs remains uncertain.

findings.summary: Several registers are reset: adbg_top module_id_reg/input_shift_reg, AXI BIU synchronizers/FSM/data_out_reg, LINT BIU address/data/write/str_sync/rdy sync, and module address/operation/bit counters. However, cross-domain reset ordering and all pending transaction/readback clear points were not fully observed.
findings.vulnerability_category: reset/clear/expose after reset or mode transition / pending debug requests, readback data registers, authorization/debug-enable state if present / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: JTAG/debug reset or mode-transition actor Operation: reset/clear/expose after reset or mode transition Object: pending debug requests, readback data registers, authorization/debug-enable state if present Expected guard: reset and lifecycle transitions should clear pending debug transactions and captured read data; debug reset should not leave authorized/enabled state or stale protected data externally shiftable Observed behavior: Several registers are reset: adbg_top module_id_reg/input_shift_reg, AXI BIU synchronizers/FSM/data_out_reg, LINT BIU address/data/write/str_sync/rdy sync, and module address/operation/bit counters. However, cross-domain reset ordering and all pending transaction/readback clear points were not fully observed. Path: trstn_i and bus-domain resets -> debug module state/registers/synchronizers -> outstanding bus requests/readback path Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Incomplete reset-domain evidence leaves residual risk of stale read data or pending transactions across reset/mode transitions.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Need full reset relationships in pulpissimo/safe_domain/soc_domain and full readback/TDO register reset behavior across module deselect and asynchronous reset mismatch.

findings.summary: AXI/LINT BIUs decode JTAG-supplied word sizes, addresses, and byte enables locally but no address-region policy, protected-register bounds, or invalid-size rejection was observed; AXI invalid/default word_size maps to 8-byte AXI size.
findings.vulnerability_category: write/read with selected size and address / bus write strobes/byte lanes and address regions reachable by debug masters / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: JTAG/debug bus master Operation: write/read with selected size and address Object: bus write strobes/byte lanes and address regions reachable by debug masters Expected guard: address alignment, byte-enable bounds, and operation size checks should prevent writes outside intended word/region and preserve downstream access policy attributes Observed behavior: BIUs compute byte enables from word_size_i and low address bits and map word_size_i to AXI aw/ar size. The AXI size default case emits 3'b011 (8 bytes). No address range comparator, deny/error path for unsupported sizes, or protected-region policy was observed in the BIU excerpts. Path: JTAG command word_size/address -> adbg_axi_biu/adbg_lint_biu be_dec/size decode -> AXI w_strb or LINT be/add Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Malformed or unrestricted debug commands may generate narrow/misaligned/unbounded accesses to downstream regions if not filtered by fabric.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Downstream fabric may impose address/size restrictions, but no such policy was provided in the observed BIU/debug path.

findings.summary: Observed top-level JTAG routing and debug wrappers route external JTAG into internal debug interfaces without a visible SoC-level lifecycle/debug-enable/pad policy gating all debug routes.
findings.vulnerability_category: route/enable debug access / external JTAG access to internal debug interfaces and buses / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: external JTAG pins/test interface Operation: route/enable debug access Object: external JTAG access to internal debug interfaces and buses Expected guard: SoC-level lifecycle/debug enable, pad mux, or safe-domain policy must mediate external JTAG reachability to advanced debug interface and internal buses Observed behavior: pulpissimo jtag_tap_top directly assigns SoC JTAG outputs from external TAP inputs and assigns external td_o from soc_tdo_i. lint_jtag_wrap directly connects TAP state/selection to LINT debug bus master outputs. adv_dbg_if exposes external JTAG pad ports and connects them into adbg_top. Searches found no debug_enable/lifecycle/auth guard signals. Path: external JTAG pins -> jtag_tap_top/adv_dbg_if/lint_jtag_wrap -> internal debug modules and bus masters Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: External pins may retain default reachability to internal debug masters and scan/readback paths in functional silicon without lifecycle mediation.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full safe_domain/pad-mux policy lines were not observed; candidate is based on visible routing plus absence of matching guard terms in the provided scope.

global_uncertainty: No closure-level missing evidence was recorded beyond the visible source scope limitations.
